title: Force profile integrating sensor
abstract: A new force-sensitive sensor structure is disclosed. The sensor (10) comprises sensor segments (20) translating applied force into electrical conductance in continuous or discrete steps. The sensor segments (20) are distributed in specified direction (16) along length of sensor (10) in such a way that the total electrical conductance sensor (10) is representative of a pressure profile applied to the sensor (10).
claims:
Claims Claims: 1 . A force-profile integrating sensor (10), in particular for use in a collision detection system of an automotive vehicle, including: a first carrier foil (22) carrying a contiguous electrically resistive layer (24), a second carrier foil (38) carrying a plurality of electrode members (40, 42, 44), the second carrier foil (38) being arranged at a specified distance from the first carrier foil (22) by means of an electrically insulating spacer (45), wherein the spacer has a plurality of active cell areas, each active cell area being defined by one out of a plurality of openings (26, 28, 30) in the spacer; and wherein the electrode members (40, 42, 44) of the plurality of electrode members (40, 42, 44) are positioned on the second carrier foil (38) to at least partially cover at least a majority of the plurality of openings (26, 28, 30) in a direction perpendicular to the second carrier foil (38) and to face the electrically resistive layer (24), and to electrically contact the electrically resistive layer (24) upon exceeding a pre-determined threshold value (32, 34, 36) for a mechanical pressure applied to one of the partially covered openings (26, 28, 30), and the plurality of openings (26, 28, 30) includes at least a first group of openings (26) having a first opening area size and a second group of openings (28) having a different second opening area size, the first opening area size and the second opening area size corresponding to a first (32) and a different second (34) pre-determined threshold value for the applied mechanical pressure, and wherein the openings of the first group of openings (26) are arranged along a specified direction (16) in a spaced manner and the openings of the second group of openings (28) are arranged along the specified direction (16) in a spaced manner, the openings of the first group of openings (26) and the openings of the second group of openings (28) being spaced from each other in a direction (18) perpendicular to the specified direction (16), the plurality of electrode members (40, 42, 44) includes a first group of electrode members (40) that at least partially cover openings of the first group of openings (26), wherein adjacently arranged electrode 

 members (40) of the first group are pairwise electrically connected by a resistor (46) having a first resistance value, and the plurality of electrode members (40, 42, 44) includes at least a second group of electrode members (42) that at least partially cover openings of the second group of openings (28), wherein adjacently arranged electrode members (42) of the second group are pairwise electrically connected by a resistor (48) having a second resistance value, at least one pair of connected electrode members (40) of the first group and at least one pair of connected electrode members (42) of the second group are aligned in the direction (18) perpendicular to the specified direction (16) to form, in combination with an opposing portion of the contiguous electrically resistive layer (24) and a portion of the spacer that is adjacently arranged in between, a sensor segment (20), and a plurality of sensor segments (20) is arranged along the specified direction (16). The sensor (10) as claimed in claim 1 , wherein the portion of the contiguous electrically resistive layer (24) of each sensor segment (20) has a rectangular shape with an aspect ratio of at least 1 .2, the longer side being arranged in the specified direction (16). The sensor (10) as claimed in claim 1 or 2, wherein the plurality of openings (26, 28, 30) further includes a third group of openings (30) having a third opening area size that is different from the first opening area size and the second opening area size, and wherein the first resistance value, the second resistance value and a third resistance value are selected according to the following formulas for equivalent conductance values (fc - 1) â G FS wherein k denotes a desired sensitivity factor, and P m denotes the m-th predetermined threshold value (32, 34, 36) in ascending order, so as to establish 

 a linear relationship between a change in electrical quantity that is measurable at the electrical terminals (12, 14) and an applied mechanical pressure level. 4. The sensor (10) as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein a shape of the openings (26, 28, 30) in the spacer is substantially rectangular with rounded corners. 5. The sensor (10) as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the electrical conductance of the electrode members (40, 42, 44) is at least twenty times the electrical conductance of a portion of the contiguous electrically resistive layer (24) contacted by one of the electrode members (40, 42, 44). 6. The sensor (10) as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, further comprising resistors (56) having a fourth resistance value that interconnect at least two electrode members (40, 42, 44) of the same group of electrode members (40, 42, 44) of two different sensor segments (20) that are adjacently arranged in the specified direction (16), for all the sensor segments (20), and at least two additional electrical terminals (52, 54) connected to the electrode members (40, 42, 44) of the same group of electrode members (40, 42, 44) of sensor segments (20) that are arranged at outmost positions with regard to the specified direction (16), wherein the at least two additional electrical terminals (52, 54) are accessible from outside the sensor (10). 7. The sensor (10) as claimed in claim 6, wherein the fourth resistance value is selected such that a time constant given by the product of a total electrical resistance resulting from summing up all the resistors (56) having a fourth resistance value and a parasitic capacitance of the sensor (10) is lower than one tenth of a specified duration of an impact intended to be sensed. 8. The sensor (10) as claimed in claim 7, wherein the fourth resistance value is chosen to be substantially higher than the respective first, second or third resistance values associated with the same group of electrode members. 9. The sensor (10) as claimed in claim 7, wherein the fourth resistance value is chosen to be equal to the respective first, second or third resistance values associated with the same group of electrode members. 10. A vehicle bumper, comprising at least one force-profile integrating sensor (10) as claimed in any one of claims 1 to 9.
